root.prompt: Which indication is this prior authorization for?

options.label: MACE-CV risk reduction (Adult) — Wegovy Injection or Tablet

citation.verbatim_quote: Authorization may be granted for the indications listed in the FDA-approved indications or compendial uses of Wegovy (semaglutide).

children.prompt: Is this initiation or continuation of therapy for MASH?

citation.verbatim_quote: Noncirrhotic Metabolic Dysfunction-Associated Steatohepatitis (MASH): DOA: 12 months

citation.verbatim_quote: The request is for Wegovy (semaglutide) injection.

citation.verbatim_quote: The requested drug is being used with a reduced-calorie diet AND increased physical activity.

citation.verbatim_quote: The request is for Wegovy (semaglutide) injection.

citation.verbatim_quote: The requested drug is being used with a reduced-calorie diet AND increased physical activity.

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has achieved or maintained a positive clinical response to the requested drug (e.g., improvement in liver function such as reduction in ALT and AST...).

children.prompt: Is this initiation or continuation of Wegovy injection for adult weight management?

citation.verbatim_quote: Reduction in excess body weight, maintenance of weight reduction long term: Initial therapy DOA: 7 months; Continuation of therapy DOA: 12 months (Wegovy Injection).

children.prompt: What is the patient's age in years?

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient is 18 years of age or older.

children.prompt: Will Wegovy be used with a reduced-calorie diet AND increased physical activity?

citation.verbatim_quote: Authorization may be granted when the requested drug will be used with a reduced-calorie diet AND increased physical activity to reduce excess body weight.

children.prompt: Has the patient participated in a comprehensive weight management program (behavioral modification, reduced-calorie diet, increased physical activity) for at least 6 months?

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has participated in a comprehensive weight management program that encourages behavioral modification, reduced-calorie diet, AND increased physical activity for at least 6 months.

children.prompt: Which BMI criterion does the patient meet?

options.label: BMI ≥ 27 kg/m² with a weight-related comorbid condition

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient meets ONE of the following: ▪ The patient has a baseline body mass index (BMI) greater than or equal to 30 kg/m² ... ▪ The patient has a baseline BMI greater than or equal to 27 kg/m² AND at least ONE weight-related comorbid condition.

children.prompt: What is the patient's baseline BMI (kg/m²)?

citation.verbatim_quote: baseline body mass index (BMI)

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has at least ONE weight-related comorbid condition (e.g., hypertension, type 2 diabetes mellitus, dyslipidemia).

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient is 18 years of age or older.

children.prompt: Has the patient completed at least 3 months of therapy with Wegovy at a stable maintenance dose?

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has completed at least 3 months of therapy with the requested drug at a stable maintenance dose.

children.prompt: Has the patient lost at least 5% of baseline body weight OR continued to maintain the initial 5% weight loss?

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has lost at least 5 percent of baseline body weight OR the patient has continued to maintain their initial 5 percent weight loss.

children.prompt: Is this initiation or continuation of Wegovy tablet for adult weight management?

citation.verbatim_quote: Wegovy Tablet: Initial therapy DOA: 6 months; Continuation of therapy DOA: 12 months.

children.prompt: Will Wegovy tablet be used in combination with a reduced-calorie diet AND increased physical activity?

citation.verbatim_quote: Wegovy tablets are indicated in combination with a reduced calorie diet and increased physical activity.

children.prompt: Which FDA-approved use applies?

options.label: MACE-CV risk reduction in adults with established CVD and obesity or overweight
Reduction in excess body weight — obesity
Reduction in excess body weight — overweight with a weight-related comorbid condition

citation.verbatim_quote: To reduce the risk of major adverse CV events (CV death, non-fatal myocardial infarction, or non-fatal stroke) in adults with established cardiovascular disease and either obesity or overweight.

children.prompt: Is the requested quantity within plan limits (≤ 30 tablets / 25 days or ≤ 90 tablets / 75 days)?

citation.verbatim_quote: Wegovy (semaglutide) tablet 1.5 mg, 4 mg, 9 mg, 25 mg — 1 Month Limit: 30 tablets / 25 days; 3 Month Limit: 90 tablets / 75 days.

children.prompt: For continuation of Wegovy tablet: the adult weight-management continuation criteria apply (3-month stable dose AND ≥5% weight loss or maintained). Does the patient meet them?

citation.verbatim_quote: Continuation of Therapy — Reduction in Excess Body Weight, Maintenance of Weight Reduction Long Term (Adult): see adult weight-management continuation criteria.

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient has established cardiovascular disease with a history of ONE of the following: Previous MI; Previous stroke; Symptomatic peripheral arterial disease (PAD), as evidenced by intermittent claudication with ankle-brachial index (ABI) less than 0.85; Prior history of revascularization.

citation.verbatim_quote: The patient does NOT have type 2 diabetes. [NOTE: Ozempic is indicated to reduce the risk of major cardiovascular events in adults with type 2 diabetes.]
